page: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-32 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Tau T2D machine family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. The configuration features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped directly to 32 physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, eliminating simultaneous multithreading overhead. It is equipped with 128 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark results indicate strong compute capabilities, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25%. The instance also demonstrates strong performance in cryptographic operations, database operations, and Redis SET operations, which reached 58.22 million operations per second. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is highly efficient at 3955.45 GB/sec, though larger uncached read and write operations fall into the average range. LLM inference speeds for small models are strong, while medium and large model inference speeds remain average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the t2d-standard-32 provides high resource density and predictable performance due to its non-shared, dedicated core allocation. The absence of simultaneous multithreading means workloads benefit from dedicated physical execution units, making it highly suitable for scale-out workloads. Recommended workloads include database operations, web serving, containerized microservices, and general-purpose business applications that require consistent CPU performance without virtualization overhead.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-32 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Tau T2D family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 x86_64 processor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with one thread per core, alongside 128 GB of RAM, offering a balanced 4 GB per vCPU. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks show strong performance in multi-core CPU execution, database operations, and Redis SET operations, alongside high memory bandwidth for small cached blocks. Large-block memory transfers and web serving throughput remain average. Due to its dedicated physical cores and lack of simultaneous multithreading overhead, the t2d-standard-32 provides predictable performance and high resource density. It is well-suited for scale-out workloads, database servers, containerized applications, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-32 is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, delivering strong multi-core CPU and database performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-32 is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 32 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 128 GB of RAM.
tagline: A dedicated 32-core AMD EPYC instance delivering predictable general-purpose performance and high resource density for scale-out workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 with 32 dedicated physical cores and no hyper-threading
Equipped with 128 GB of system memory providing 4 GB of RAM per vCPU
Strong multi-core CPU, cryptographic, and database benchmark performance
High memory bandwidth for small cached blocks and fast Redis operations
Optimized for scale-out workloads, database servers, and containerized microservices
